back_translation: The descendants of Israel again did evil in the sight of the LORD, after Ehud died.
The LORD sold them into the hand of Jabin king of Canaan, who ruled in Hazor. The commander of his army was Sisera, who lived in Harosheth-hagoyim.
The descendants of Israel cried out to the LORD, because Jabin had nine hundred iron chariots, and he had harshly oppressed the descendants of Israel for twenty years.
Deborah was a prophetess at that time, the wife of Lappidoth; she was judging Israel.
She used to sit under the Palm of Deborah, between Ramah and Bethel in the hill country of Ephraim, and the descendants of Israel came up to her for judgment.

She sent and called Barak son of Abinoam from Kedesh in Naphtali, and said to him, 'The LORD God of Israel has commanded: Go and gather your forces at Mount Tabor, and take with you ten thousand men from the descendants of Naphtali and the descendants of Zebulun.'
'I will draw out Sisera the commander of Jabin's army, along with his chariots and his troops, to the River Kishon to meet you, and I will deliver him into your hand.'
Barak said to her, 'If you will go with me, I will go; but if you will not go with me, I will not go.'

She answered, 'I will certainly go with you, but the road you are taking will not bring you honor, because the LORD will sell Sisera into the hand of a woman.' Then Deborah arose and went with Barak to Kedesh.
Barak summoned Zebulun and Naphtali to Kedesh, and ten thousand men followed him up, and Deborah also went up with him.
Now Heber the Kenite had separated himself from the Kenites, the descendants of Hobab the father-in-law of Moses, and had pitched his tent near the oak at Zaanannim, which is near Kedesh.
When they reported to Sisera that Barak son of Abinoam had gone up Mount Tabor,
Sisera summoned all his chariots, nine hundred iron chariots, and all the troops who were with him, from Harosheth-hagoyim to the River Kishon.

Deborah said to Barak, 'Arise, for this is the day when the LORD has delivered Sisera into your hand. Has not the LORD gone out before you?' Barak went down from Mount Tabor with ten thousand men following him.
The LORD threw Sisera and all his chariots and all his army into confusion by the edge of the sword before Barak. Sisera dismounted from his chariot and fled on foot.
Barak pursued the chariots and the army as far as Harosheth-hagoyim, and the whole army of Sisera fell by the edge of the sword; not even one was left.
Sisera had fled on foot to the tent of Jael the wife of Heber the Kenite, because there was peace between Jabin king of Hazor and the house of Heber the Kenite.
Jael went out to greet Sisera and said to him, 'Come in, my lord; come in to me, do not be afraid.' He entered her tent, and she covered him with a blanket.
He said to her, 'Please give me a little water to drink, for I am thirsty.' She opened a skin of milk, gave him a drink, and covered him.
He said to her, 'Stand at the entrance of the tent, and if anyone comes and asks you, Is there a man here? — say, No.'

But Jael wife of Heber took a tent peg and held a hammer in her hand. She came quietly to him and drove the peg into his temple, and it went down into the ground while he was fast asleep from exhaustion; and he died.

Behold, Barak came pursuing Sisera, and Jael went out to greet him, and said to him, 'Come, and I will show you the man you are looking for.' He came to her, and behold, Sisera lay dead, with the peg in his temple.
On that day God subdued Jabin king of Canaan before the descendants of Israel.
The hand of the descendants of Israel grew stronger and stronger against Jabin king of Canaan, until they destroyed Jabin king of Canaan.
